⮚ Managing Taxes: 
● The iAmX allows users to set up different tax rates based on location (state, country, 
city), product type (some items might be under tax-exemption), or customer type (e.g. 
tax-exempt organizations) - Need to verify

● Option selection conditions 
▪ If the minimum selection quantity is “0” then option sets become optional 
while placing an order 
▪ If the minimum selection quantity is “1” or more, then option sets become 
mandatory while placing an order 
▪ If the maximum selection quantity is “1” then the option set buttons will 
be displayed in radio buttons 
• This system empowers retailers to manage product options effectively. Making 
some options  optional  streamlines  checkout  for customers  who  only  want  the 
basics 
• Requiring  other  options  ensures  complete  orders  for  customizable  products, 
reducing  processing  delays  and  errors.  Using  radio  buttons  for  single-choice 
options simplifies the customer experience and prevents conflicting selections, 
leading to fewer returns and happier customers.

from their original purchase, and the refund amount is calculated 
proportionally based on the price of the returned items. 
▪ After a returned product is processed, the inventory is 
automatically updated. Details of these inventory changes can be 
viewed in the product inventory logs on the organization or store 
dashboard.

● Non-Refundable Returns 
▪ Exchange or Replacement: Instead of a monetary refund, the 
customer receives a different item in exchange for the returned 
product or a replacement for a defective or incorrect item. This 
often occurs due to size/color changes or product defects. 
▪ Inventory Adjustment: The returned item is processed, and 
inventory is updated to reflect the return. The 
exchange/replacement is recorded as an inventory adjustment, 
not a financial transaction, with the new item added to stock 
and the returned item removed (or marked as damaged). 
● Tracking exchanges and replacements separately in retail software is 
essential for accurate inventory, detailed transaction history, and 
reliable financial reporting. This also improves customer service and 
facilitates return policy enforcement.
